assumption_text: The denominator footprint for the full estimate is Jenin refugee camp plus immediate battle environs, not Jenin governorate.
rationale: The core fighting and the best numerator sources are camp-specific. Using the whole governorate would dilute civilian exposure with large uninvolved populations.

assumption_text: Average exposed Palestinian civilians are modeled as 4,000 (low), 8,000 (best), and 14,000 (high) over the 3-11 April battle window.
rationale: HRW gives a pre-battle ceiling of more than 14,000 civilians; the UN report records an Israeli-source account that about 11,000 departed and that as many as 4,000 remained throughout.

assumption_text: Average exposed Palestinian organized armed personnel are modeled as 80 (low), 100 (best), and 300 (high) over the 3-11 April battle window.
rationale: HRW relays media estimates of 80-100 militants; the UN report preserves an Israeli account of 'hundreds of gunmen', which is used only as a conservative upper-bound scenario.

assumption_text: The three Palestinian deaths with unresolved status are excluded from the base DR rather than silently allocated.
rationale: The prompt requires strict separation of unknown-status deaths unless a sensitivity allocation is run explicitly.

assumption_text: Person-month denominators use a 9-day combat window from 3 April through 11 April 2002 inclusive.
rationale: HRW dates the operation's launch to 3 April; the UN report says the last Palestinian militants surrendered on 11 April.

assumption_text: The published DR is side-specific for Palestinian victims of Israeli forces; Israeli military deaths are recorded separately but not blended into the same DR.
rationale: A blended all-victim DR would force unlike exposure footprints. Israeli military victims in the camp have no comparable Israeli civilian co-presence denominator inside the same footprint.
